findings.evidence: `rg` shows `hausdorff_bernstein_widder` at lines 759-765, but lines 770-775 repeat `hausdorff_bernstein_widder_of_exists_of_laplaceTransform` with the same existence/easy-direction inputs.
findings.issue: `hausdorff_bernstein_widder_unique` rebuilds the non-unique HBW theorem instead of deriving from the public `hausdorff_bernstein_widder`.

findings.evidence: `Basic.lean:368` has `IsClosedCompletelyMonotone.of_isCompletelyMonotone`; `BernsteinTheorem.lean:768` has the general unique theorem; lines 781-784 duplicate existence plus `laplaceTransform_unique`.
findings.issue: `existsUnique_representsLaplace_of_isCompletelyMonotone` manually combines strong-predicate existence and uniqueness although the closed-half-line unique theorem already gives this special case.

summary: The closed-half-line HBW statements are at the natural level, but the unique-existence layer still duplicates general assembly instead of deriving the special forms from the already-proved general theorem.
